title: Transport Node Data Plane Interface Errors and Drops
spl:
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:tn_iface" earliest=-24h
| eval bad=rx_errors+tx_errors+rx_drops+tx_drops
| stats sum(bad) as issues max(rx_errors) as max_rx max(tx_errors) as max_tx by transport_node, pnic
| where issues > 100 OR max_rx>0 OR max_tx>0
| sort -issues
| table transport_node, pnic, issues, max_rx, max_tx
description: Overlay GENEVE depends on clean physical NICs and switch ports. Rising errors or drops on TN N-VDS or VDS uplinks manifest as intermittent VM connectivity and false-positive firewall symptoms. Isolating TN-level drops speeds root cause between server, rack, and fabric teams.
value: Overlay GENEVE depends on clean physical NICs and switch ports. Rising errors or drops on TN N-VDS or VDS uplinks manifest as intermittent VM connectivity and false-positive firewall symptoms. Isolating TN-level drops speeds root cause between server, rack, and fabric teams.
grandmaExplanation: Overlay GENEVE depends on clean physical NICs and switch ports ‚Äî so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
implementation: (1) Collect per-pnic counters via NSX API on 60s interval. (2) Baseline known noisy lab hosts. (3) Correlate with overlay loss diagnostics (UC-18.2.9).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `vmware_nsx_addon`, vSphere metrics optional.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vmware` `sourcetype="vmware:nsx:tn_iface"` with fields `transport_node`, `pnic`, `rx_errors`, `tx_errors`, `rx_drops`, `tx_drops`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Collect per-pnic counters via NSX API on 60s interval. (2) Baseline known noisy lab hosts. (3) Correlate with overlay loss diagnostics (UC-18.2.9).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:tn_iface" earliest=-24h
| eval bad=rx_errors+tx_errors+rx_drops+tx_drops
| stats sum(bad) as issues max(rx_errors) as max_rx max(tx_errors) as max_tx by transport_node, pnic
| where issues > 100 OR max_rx>0 OR max_tx>0
| sort -issues
| table transport_node, pnic, issues, max_rx, max_tx
```

Understanding this SPL

**Transport Node Data Plane Interface Errors and Drops** ‚Äî Overlay GENEVE depends on clean physical NICs and switch ports. Rising errors or drops on TN N-VDS or VDS uplinks manifest as intermittent VM connectivity and false-positive firewall symptoms. Isolating TN-level drops speeds root cause between server, rack, and fabric teams.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vmware` `sourcetype="vmware:nsx:tn_iface"` with fields `transport_node`, `pnic`, `rx_errors`, `tx_errors`, `rx_drops`, `tx_drops`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `vmware_nsx_addon`, vSphere metrics optional. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vmware; **sourcetype**: vmware:nsx:tn_iface. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=vmware, sourcetype="vmware:nsx:tn_iface", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **bad** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by transport_node, pnic** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where issues > 100 OR max_rx>0 OR max_tx>0` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Transport Node Data Plane Interface Errors and Drops**): table transport_node, pnic, issues, max_rx, max_tx


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (issues by TN), Table (worst pnics), Single value (TNs with errors).
visualization: Bar chart (issues by TN), Table (worst pnics), Single value (TNs with errors).
